query: Intel Foundry 18A-P risk production entry date 2026 performance thermal characteristics customer timeline

{"query":"Intel Foundry 18A-P risk production entry date 2026 performance thermal characteristics customer timeline","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1662,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://newsroom.intel.com/intel-foundry/intel-foundry-details-process-milestones-future-innovation-at-vlsi-symposium","title":"Intel Foundry Details Process Milestones and Future Innovation at VLSI Symposium - Intel Newsroom","content":"Intel 18A, Intel Foundry's leading-edge process node, entered into production in 2025.
Intel 18 A-P now in risk production with higher performance, enhanced thermal characteristics and design rule compatibility with Intel 18A
other risks and uncertainties described in this report, our 2025 Form 10-K and our other filings with the SEC."},{"url":"https://futurumgroup.com/insights/will-intel-18a-p-risk-production-bring-external-foundry-customers-through-the-door/","title":"Intel 18A-P Risk Production: Performance Gains","content":"Intel Foundry has moved Intel 18A-P, the first performance enhancement to its 18A node, into risk production, delivering 9% higher performance or 18% lower power along with a new Power Boost transistor.
Intel 18A-P, the first performance enhancement in the Intel 18A family, has entered risk production on schedule, running lead products in Intel’s Oregon and Arizona fabs.
These include a new dual-contact “Power Boost” device, 20–40% improved thermal resistance, 10–30% improved via resistance at performance-critical layers, a mobility enhancement via PMOS strain engineering, new low-power and high-performance transistor options, and a new fifth logic Vt pair between ULVT and LVT.
The Intel 18A-P milestone lands exactly when Intel told customers it would last year, with lead products already running in both Oregon and Arizona.
Intel reinforced that lead at VLSI with quantified data: an invited talk detailed roughly 11% routed-area reduction, a 10x dynamic voltage droop reduction, and up to 6% frequency uplift or greater than 15% dynamic-power reduction versus comparable frontside interconnect."},{"url":"https://gulfsqas.com/expert-time/Intel-Foundry-Takes-Next-Step-18AP-Process-Enters-Risk-Production-Phase-32-11064","title":"Intel Foundry Takes Next Step: 18A-P Process Enters Risk Production Phase - ROA Comparison","content":"Intel Foundry has announced that its Intel 18A-P process technology has moved into the risk production phase, signaling progress in the company’s advanced semiconductor manufacturing roadmap.
The entry of Intel 18A-P into risk production carries several implications for the semiconductor landscape.
Second, this milestone aligns with Intel’s stated goal to offer leading-edge manufacturing services to external customers by 2025-2026.
Intel Foundry Takes Next Step: 18A-P Process Enters Risk Production Phase Investors often test different approaches before settling on a strategy."},{"url":"https://www.insiderfinance.io/news/intel-18a-p-risk-production-confirmed","title":"Intel 18A-P Risk Production Confirmed | InsiderFinance","content":"Jun 17, 2026 — Intel (INTC) said on June 16, 2026, that Intel 18A‑P, a performance-enhanced variant of its leading-edge 18A process, has entered risk ...Read more
Intel (INTC) said on June 16, 2026, that Intel 18A‑P, a performance-enhanced variant of its leading-edge 18A process, has entered risk production.
Intel said in a press release on June 16 that 18A‑P has entered risk production, meeting the timeline first shared with customers and partners last year.
Intel said 18A‑P delivers roughly 9% higher performance at iso-power or about 18% lower power at iso-performance compared with 18A.
The release highlights enhanced thermal characteristics and expanded design flexibility.
Technical summaries estimate thermal resistance improvements of 20–40% and via resistance gains of 10–30%.
Intel’s prior quarterly guidance projected second-quarter revenue between $13.8 billion and $14.8 billion, compared with a consensus of $13.07 billion.
Apple world's most valuable company reclaimed on July 17, 2026, as Apple passed Nvidia and positioning shifted from AI hardware to consumer execution."}]}

Intel 18A-P risk production confirms roadmap progress and strengthens Intel foundry positioning by signaling readiness for internal and external customers. * Intel 18A-P entered risk production, meeting the timeline first shared with customers and partners. * The milestone signals readiness to serve Intel products and external foundry customers. Intel (INTC) said on June 16, 2026, that Intel 18A‑P, a performance-enhanced variant of its leading-edge 18A process, has entered risk production. Management said this milestone validates its roadmap and signals readiness to serve both internal products and external foundry customers. Intel said in a press release on June 16 that 18A‑P has entered risk production, meeting the timeline first shared with customers and partners last year. The release describes 18A‑P as the first performance enhancement in the Intel 18A family and reconfirms that 18A remains the company’s leading-edge node, on track for production in 2025. It positions 18A‑P to serve both Intel’s processors and external foundry customers, strengthening Intel foundry’s market positioning.","score":0.8851659,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://futurumgroup.com/insights/will-intel-18a-p-risk-production-bring-external-foundry-customers-through-the-door","title":"Intel 18A-P Risk Production: Performance Gains","content":"Get organization-wide instant access to market sizing and decision-maker data in a self-serve subscription model with analyst support. # Futurum Research 2026: Key Issues and Predictions. With full design-rule compatibility, on-schedule delivery, and a compelling PPA story, Intel 18A-P positions Intel Foundry to enable external customers to adopt the process and commit design starts on a leading-edge node backed by a credible long-term roadmap. **The News:** At the 2026 VLSI Symposium, Intel Foundry announced that Intel 18A-P has entered risk production, meeting the timeline first shared with customers and partners a year ago. This is the kind of advantage that is hard to copy quickly, and it reinforces Futurum’s standing view that Intel Foundry leads the industry in combining backside power delivery with gate-all-around transistors.","score":0.88372874,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/semidoped_daily-update-june-17th-2026-activity-7473098247943651329-EuBX","title":"Intel 18A-P Enters Risk Production on Schedule","content":"Intel 18A-P, the first performance enhancement in the Intel 18A family, has entered risk production on schedule, hitting the timeline Intel","score":0.8386933,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://newsroom.intel.com/intel-foundry/intel-foundry-details-process-milestones-future-innovation-at-vlsi-symposium","title":"Intel Foundry Details Process Milestones and Future ...","content":"# Intel Foundry Details Process Milestones and Future Innovation at VLSI Symposium. Intel 18A, Intel Foundry's leading-edge process node, entered into production in 2025. Santa Clara, California, June 16, 2026 – At the 2026 VLSI Symposium, Intel Foundry provided an update on its process roadmap and long-term innovation investments. It shared that Intel 18A‑P, the first performance enhancement in the Intel 18A family, has entered risk production, meeting the timeline first shared with customers and partners last year. “Our updates and presentations at VLSI signal to Intel Foundry customers and partners that we are fully committed to leading edge process innovation over the long term,” said Naga Chandrasekaran, executive vice president and general manager of Intel Foundry. “This is a journey, and while we have more work ahead, we appreciate the opportunity to share the progress we are making with Intel 18A-P and our longer-range R&D.”. **Intel 18A**‑**P updates at VLSI**.","score":0.82048416,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.techtimes.com/articles/318568/20260619/intel-moves-18a-p-risk-production-hitting-timeline-it-promised-customers.htm","title":"Intel Moves 18A-P Into Risk Production, Hitting the Timeline It Promised Customers","content":"# Intel Moves 18A-P Into Risk Production, Hitting the Timeline It Promised Customers. #### For a company dogged by delays, hitting the date it gave customers last year is as notable as the specs. Intel said on June 16 that 18A-P, an enhanced version of its 1.8nm-class 18A process, has entered risk production — on the timeline it gave customers and partners last year. Risk production is the stage at which a process is frozen and run on production tooling so that early customers can commit their designs — at their own risk — before high-volume manufacturing yields are fully proven. Risk production is a stage between development and high-volume manufacturing in which the process design is locked and run on production equipment, letting early customers commit their chip designs before yields are fully proven — hence \"at their own risk.\" It signals that a node is ready for customers to build on, but it does not by itself indicate production volume, final yields, or commercial shipments.","score":0.77792513,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.52,"request_id":"ca978e52-9368-4b7a-9109-61fdb141a838"}
